text: The darkness and sparse lighting instill in me a sense of solitude and quiet curiosity. The environment feels somewhat stark and impersonal, the kind of place that exists more for function than comfort. Shadows and empty spaces spark existential musings—what is the intended purpose here, and who do these silent halls serve when no one is around? Despite this, a sense of calm persists, as if I am allowed to reflect freely, unobserved and unhurried.
